title: flutter_riverpod

sections.markdown: `flutter_riverpod` is the Flutter binding for Riverpod — compile-safe, context-free DI + state. Wrap the app in `ProviderScope`; widgets extend `ConsumerWidget` (or use `HookConsumer`).

Riverpod fixes Provider's biggest issues: no BuildContext dependency (test-friendly), multiple providers of the same type, and compile-time safety. `ProviderScope.overrides` swaps providers for widget/integration tests without touching widgets.
Calling `ref.watch` in an event handler → runtime error. `watch` is for build; use `ref.read` in callbacks, and `ref.listen` for side effects that shouldn't rebuild.
Use `.autoDispose` for provider lifecycles tied to a screen; use `family` for parameterized providers (e.g. `userProvider(id)`). The codegen (`@riverpod`) removes even more boilerplate.

flashcards.back: watch subscribes (build only); read is one-off (callbacks); listen runs side effects on change without rebuilding.
Wrap in a ProviderScope with `overrides` mapping providers to fakes.

quiz.explain: watch is only allowed in build.

quiz.q: To override providers in a test…
Providers tied to a screen's lifecycle use…
quiz.explain: autoDispose cleans up when nothing is listening.

challenges.text: FutureProvider fetching user data and rendering AsyncValue.when.
NotifierProvider driving a search screen with debounce + cancellation.
Widget test overriding providers with fakes and asserting rebuild behavior.

interviewQuestions: How does Riverpod improve on Provider?
